task: I need to send a short note to a subcontractor about a missed step on a job. What would be a clear way to word it so the message is easy to understand and can be used right away?

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a short, clear version you can use right away:

**Subject:** Missed Step on Job

[Subcontractor Name],  
We identified a missed step on the job that needs to be addressed. Please review the work and correct this issue as soon as possible. Let us know when it has been completed.

Thank you,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also give you:
- a **firmer version**
- a **more formal version**
- or a version that **doesn’t sound accusatory** while still protecting you.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but too soft. I’d want it tighter and more specific. Something like:

[Subcontractor Name],
A required step was missed on this job. Stop and correct it before any further work continues. Confirm when it’s fixed.

[Your Name]

If you want, make it a little firmer and add the exact step that was missed. That’s better for the record.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a tighter version you can use right away:

**[Subcontractor Name],**  
A required step was missed on this job. Stop work on the affected area and correct it before any further work continues. Confirm when it has been fixed.

**[Your Name]**

If you want, I can make it:
- **firmer**
- **more detailed for the record**
- or **shorter and more direct**

user_simulator.text: Better. Still a little generic though. I’d want the missed step named in the note, not just “required step.” Something like rebar, inspection, layout, whatever it was. That way nobody can play dumb later. Give me the firmer one with the exact step in it.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a firmer version with the exact step named:

**[Subcontractor Name],**  
The **[specific missed step]** was not completed on this job. Stop work in the affected area and correct this before any further work continues. Confirm once it has been fixed.

**[Your Name]**

If you want, I can also give you:
- a **more formal version**
- a **slightly harsher version**
- or a version that includes **date/job name for the record**

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a little generic in the middle. I’d want it to say the missed step has to be completed and checked before work resumes, not just “correct this.” And throw in the job name or date if you’re going to make it stick for the record.

Like:

[Subcontractor Name],
The [specific missed step] was not completed on [job name/date]. Stop work in the affected area. Complete and verify that step before any further work continues. Confirm when it’s fixed.

[Your Name]

turns.agent_response:
Yes — that version is solid. Here’s a tightened version you can send right away:

**[Subcontractor Name],**  
The **[specific missed step]** was not completed on **[job name/date]**. Stop work in the affected area. Complete and verify that step before any further work continues. Confirm when it’s fixed.

**[Your Name]**

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little **firmer**
- more **formal**
- or turn it into a **text message version**

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That works. Tight, specific, and it covers the record.

The only thing I’d keep in mind is if I’m sending it, I’d put the exact step in there and the job name or date so there’s no wiggle room. But the wording itself is solid.
